title: acer miyabei var. shibatae
intro: Acer miyabei var. shibatae, also known as the Japanese maple, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Sapindaceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used in Eastern medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, due to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Acer miyabei var. shibatae include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and potentially exerting antiviral and antibacterial activities. In herbal preparations, the leaves, bark, and roots of the plant are used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
